summary: Executed comprehensive pytest suite (36 tests) against Samaadhaan API v2 covering health, OTP, policy, office auth, ticket CRUD + routing/scoping, LLM translation, LLM classification, resolve, aging + auto-escalation, manual escalate-auth, analytics, search/pagination, CSV export, audit RBAC, public history, and the new /api/inbox + /api/inbox/{id}/mark-read internal-inbox flow. All 36 tests PASSED against the live public URL. LLM (real Hindi translation via Emergent LLM key) and auto-classify (urgent priority) both verified working end-to-end.

minor.issue: POST /api/tickets is unauthenticated and can create tickets on any policy_no by anyone. Consider tying to a verified-OTP session or origin check to prevent spam.
Office passwords stored + compared in PLAINTEXT (see seed_data). Use bcrypt hashes before any production use.
Test-only endpoint reachable in prod-shaped API with just office-auth; consider gating behind admin-only or a DEBUG flag.

This route has NO auth dependency and takes an optional payload=None — anyone hitting POST /api/tickets/{id}/escalate directly can escalate any ticket. The /escalate-auth variant is authenticated, but the raw /escalate is public. Recommend removing the public route or adding Depends(current_office).
Endpoint returns embedded 'audio_base64' inside the 'ticket' object which can be large. Consider stripping audio_base64 from the ticket join to keep responses light.

action_items: Add auth dependency to POST /api/tickets/{id}/escalate (currently unauthenticated) OR remove it and keep only /escalate-auth.
Hash office passwords (bcrypt/argon2) instead of plaintext equality check.
Consider protecting POST /api/tickets with an OTP-verification token so customers can't be impersonated.
Trim heavy fields (audio_base64) from the ticket enrichment on /api/inbox to reduce payload size.
Move /simulate-aging behind admin-only or a debug flag.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 660 lines — approaching split threshold. Extract routers per-domain (auth, tickets, inbox, analytics, llm) into separate modules before it grows further.
POST /api/tickets/{id}/escalate lacks Depends(current_office) — auth bypass risk.
Plaintext password compare in /api/auth/office/login — production risk.
TicketCreate endpoint has no rate limiting/OTP-session binding — spoofing risk.
Analytics loops in-memory over up to 2000 tickets; fine for MVP, should move to Mongo aggregation as data grows.
LLM call has no timeout; a stalled provider will hang requests. Wrap _chat_send in asyncio.wait_for(...).
audio_base64 stored inside Ticket doc (potentially large blob in Mongo). Consider GridFS or object storage.

Notifications collection is used both as an event sink AND as a real inbox — a 'read_at' write from any authed office on any notif_id (mark-read) has no ownership check, so office 940000 could theoretically mark 670100's mail as read if it knew the id.
seed_data_creation: None new — used app's built-in seeded policies + offices. Tests do create additional ticket + notification rows in Mongo (not cleaned up, by design for regression traceability).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py runs in parallel via pytest-xdist (2 workers) and completes in ~8s. All 36 tests are stable. LLM translation depends on live Emergent LLM key — a network blip may flake test_hindi_translation / test_auto_classify_urgent; retry once if seen. The /api/tickets/{id}/escalate route (without -auth) is intentionally NOT covered as it is an auth-bypass suspect — flagged for main agent. Notification mark-read has no ownership check — noted, not tested destructively.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing tests. Verified INBOX feature works end-to-end: creating a ticket for policy 67010023456789012001 immediately produces an email notification to office670100@newindia.co.in, which appears in GET /api/inbox for the 670100 office with the 'ticket' object joined, and does NOT leak to the 940000 office's inbox.
